primary_focus: useEffectEvent for reading latest values in effects without re-triggering subscriptions

effect_event_signals.signal: useEffectEvent for notification handling in the event subscription
effect_event_signals.trigger: Requirement 8: changing notification settings must not cause the event subscription to be re-established
effect_event_signals.correct: The effect that subscribes to the WebSocket connection uses useEffectEvent to define the notification handler. The effect itself depends only on the connection (connect/disconnect), not on sound/toast/keyword/severity settings. The effect event reads the latest settings values when called, without being listed in the effect's dependency array.
effect_event_signals.example_structure: const onEvent = useEffectEvent((event: ActivityEvent) => { if (matchesKeyword(event, keyword) && meetsMinSeverity(event, minSeverity)) { if (soundEnabled) playNotificationSound(); if (toastEnabled) showToast(event, toastDuration); } addEventToFeed(event); }); useEffect(() => { return connect(onEvent); }, []);
effect_event_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: Including all notification settings in the useEffect dependency array, causing the subscription to disconnect and reconnect every time any setting changes — this means missed events during reconnection
effect_event_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: Using a ref to hold the latest settings and reading it inside the effect callback. This works but is the manual pattern that useEffectEvent replaces — it is error-prone (forgetting to update the ref) and shows lack of React 19.2 knowledge.
effect_event_signals.anti_pattern_tier3: Disabling the exhaustive-deps lint rule with eslint-disable to exclude settings from the dependency array. This hides the real problem and risks stale closures in other scenarios.

effect_event_signals.signal: useEffectEvent for auto-save with configurable interval
effect_event_signals.trigger: Requirements 14-16: auto-save notes at configurable interval, must use latest content
effect_event_signals.correct: The auto-save effect depends on the interval (to reset the timer when interval changes). The actual save logic is an effect event that reads the latest notes content without being a dependency. Changing the interval restarts the timer. Changing the notes content does NOT restart the timer — the next save will pick up the latest content.
effect_event_signals.example_structure: const saveNotes = useEffectEvent(() => { localStorage.setItem('notes', notes); setLastSaved(new Date()); }); useEffect(() => { const id = setInterval(saveNotes, interval); return () => clearInterval(id); }, [interval]);
effect_event_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: Including notes in the useEffect dependency array — this restarts the timer on every keystroke, defeating the purpose of interval-based saving
effect_event_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: Using a ref for notes and reading ref.current inside the interval callback. Works but is the manual workaround for useEffectEvent.
effect_event_signals.anti_pattern_tier3: Using a separate useEffect to sync notes to a ref, then reading the ref in the interval effect. Over-engineered version of the ref workaround.

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: bufferedEventCount
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.trigger: Requirement 6: count badge on Pause button
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Derived from the buffered events array length
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Separate counter state incremented via useEffect

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: toastList filtering/limiting
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Toast state is a managed array (via state), but the 5-item limit is enforced in the handler that adds toasts, not via a useEffect that trims the array after it grows

legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: useEffect(() => { return connect(onEvent); }, []) where onEvent is a useEffectEvent
legitimate_effect_uses.note: This is THE core effect. It should have an empty or minimal dependency array. Notification settings must NOT be dependencies.

legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: useEffect with setInterval, depending on the interval setting only. Save logic is a useEffectEvent.
legitimate_effect_uses.note: The effect should restart when interval changes, but NOT when notes content changes.

legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: useEffect or setTimeout per toast to remove it after the configured duration

event_handler_patterns.correct: onClick handler toggles paused state. Event subscription stays connected. Feed rendering checks paused state.
onClick handler removes the toast from toast state array

accessibility_checkpoints: Toast area has role='status' or aria-live='polite'
Toggle buttons (Sound, Toast, Pause) have aria-pressed
Dismiss buttons on toasts have accessible labels
Severity filter dropdown has associated label
Event type badges use text labels, not just color
